question: Provide your approved Information Security Policy and topic-specific policies, showing version, approval date, and evidence of management sign-off and annual review.
Submit documentation defining information security roles and responsibilities (e.g. RACI or org chart) and evidence these have been communicated and acknowledged.
Demonstrate how conflicting duties are segregated. Provide a SoD matrix or access review showing no single individual can request, approve, and implement a sensitive change.
Provide evidence that management requires all personnel to apply information security per policy (e.g. signed acknowledgements, performance objectives).
Demonstrate how you collect and action threat intelligence. Provide a sample threat intel report or feed export and evidence it informed a control decision.
Submit your asset inventory export showing owners, classification, and last-reviewed date for information and associated assets.
Provide your Acceptable Use Policy and evidence of user acknowledgement (signed forms or e-acknowledgement export).
Provide your information classification scheme and an example showing how assets are labelled per scheme.
Provide your information transfer policy and evidence of secure transfer mechanisms (e.g. encrypted file transfer config, DLP rules, transfer agreements).
Provide your Access Control Policy and a sample access-rights export from a key system showing role-based assignment.
Demonstrate the joiner/mover/leaver identity lifecycle. Pull a user-provisioning and de-provisioning report from your identity provider.
Provide your password/secret management policy and configuration evidence (e.g. password policy export, secrets-vault config).
Submit evidence of a recent user access review for a critical system, including reviewer, date, and remediation of revoked access.
Provide your supplier security policy and a sample supplier register showing risk tier and assigned owner.
Submit a sample supplier contract or DPA showing the information security clauses (confidentiality, right to audit, breach notification).
Provide evidence of monitoring supplier service delivery (e.g. SLA reports, review-meeting minutes, SOC 2 report review notes).
Submit your cloud service security policy and configuration evidence for a key cloud provider (e.g. CSP security settings, shared-responsibility mapping).
Provide your information security incident response plan showing roles, severity classification, and escalation paths.
Submit a post-incident report for a recent incident showing response actions, timeline, and root cause.
Provide evidence that incident lessons learned drive improvement (e.g. action items tracked to closure from a post-mortem).
Provide your information security continuity plan and evidence of its last test (BCP/DR test report).
Provide your register of applicable legal, statutory, regulatory, and contractual requirements with assigned owners.
Provide your records retention schedule and evidence records are protected from loss and unauthorized access.
Provide the report from the most recent independent review or internal audit of information security.
Demonstrate periodic compliance checks against policies and standards. Provide a sample compliance review or attestation.
Provide evidence of background screening for a recent hire (redacted check results or screening-completion record).
Provide your security awareness training records showing completion rates and content for the current cycle.
Submit a sample signed NDA/confidentiality agreement and the register tracking signed agreements.
Provide your remote working policy and supporting technical controls (e.g. VPN config, device posture requirements).
Demonstrate the mechanism for personnel to report security events. Provide a screenshot of the reporting channel and a sample submitted report.

Provide evidence of physical security perimeters for facilities housing information assets (e.g. floor plans, badge-zone diagrams). If cloud-hosted, provide the subservice organization physical security controls via CSP bridge letter.
Submit a sample physical access log and the entry-control configuration (badge reader settings or access-list export). For cloud, refer to CSP SOC 2 physical controls.
Demonstrate your clear desk/clear screen policy and evidence of enforcement (e.g. screen-lock MDM config or compliance report from Kandji).
Submit certificates of destruction or secure-wipe records for recently decommissioned equipment containing data. Include MDM wipe-confirmation records.
Provide endpoint configuration evidence (MDM policy export from Kandji) showing encryption, screen-lock, patch enforcement, and EDR deployment on managed devices.
Submit a list of privileged accounts and evidence of their review, including how privileged access is approved and time-bound. Pull from identity provider and AWS IAM.
Demonstrate access controls on source code repositories. Provide a repo access-permissions export and branch-protection configuration from GitHub.
Provide evidence of MFA enforcement. Pull an MFA-coverage report from your identity provider showing enrolment for privileged and remote users.
Provide your anti-malware coverage report from your EDR console (CrowdStrike) showing deployment percentage, prevention-policy configuration, and recent detection/response actions.
Submit your latest vulnerability scan results from your EDR/vulnerability tool (CrowdStrike Spotlight or equivalent) and evidence of remediation SLAs being met (ticket export with close dates).
Provide your secure baseline/hardening standard for a key platform and evidence of enforcement (e.g. CIS-benchmark scan results, Kandji blueprint config).
Submit your DLP policy configuration and a sample DLP alert report showing rules in effect.
Provide your backup policy and evidence of a recent successful backup and restore test (backup job logs and restore-test report).
Provide your logging standard and a sample log export showing security-relevant events are captured for a key system (CloudTrail, Okta syslog, GitHub audit log).
Submit evidence of security monitoring (SIEM dashboard screenshots, alert rules, and a sample investigated alert from CrowdStrike or equivalent).
Submit network security control evidence including firewall and WAF configuration (Cloudflare WAF rules, AWS security groups) and network segmentation diagram.
Demonstrate network segregation. Provide VLAN/subnet design, Cloudflare zone configuration, and firewall rules enforcing separation between production and non-production.
Provide your web filtering/proxy configuration showing blocked categories (Cloudflare Gateway or equivalent) and a sample blocked-access report.
Submit your cryptography policy and enforcement evidence (TLS configuration scan of public endpoints, encryption-at-rest settings for S3/RDS, certificate inventory from ACM).
Provide your secure SDLC standard and evidence of security gates in the pipeline (Semgrep SAST integration config and a sample scan result).
Provide your secure coding standard and evidence of SAST integration in CI/CD (Semgrep pipeline config and a sample finding with remediation).
Submit results from security testing in development and acceptance (SAST reports from Semgrep, DAST results, or the latest penetration test report).
Demonstrate separation of development, test, and production environments (env0 environment inventory showing prod/staging separation, and access controls per environment).
Provide your change management procedure and a sample change record showing request, approval, testing, and deployment (GitHub PR with review approvals and env0 deployment run).
